seoTitle: How to Link to Another Jira Ticket
metaDescription: Learn how to create a link between two Jira tickets to show relationships like dependencies or duplicates.
intro: Sometimes two Jira tickets need to reference each other without one being a subtask or child of the other. This guide covers how to add that kind of link and choose the right relationship type.

steps.heading: Open the ticket you want to link from
steps.body: Go to the issue's detail view where the link option is available.

steps.heading: Click the link issue option
steps.body: Find the link icon or menu item used to connect this ticket to another one.

steps.heading: Pick the appropriate link type
steps.body: Choose a relationship such as 'blocks', 'is blocked by', 'duplicates', or 'relates to' depending on how the tickets connect.

steps.heading: Search for the target ticket
steps.body: Type the ticket's key or summary to find it, then select it to complete the link.

steps.heading: Confirm the link appears on both tickets
steps.body: Check that the link shows up correctly, with the appropriate inverse relationship, on the other ticket as well.

faq.question: What link types does Jira support?
faq.answer: Common types include 'blocks', 'is blocked by', 'duplicates', 'is duplicated by', 'relates to', and others depending on your instance's configuration.

faq.question: Can I link tickets across different projects?
faq.answer: Yes, issue links work across projects as long as you have visibility into both tickets.

faq.question: Does linking a ticket change its workflow?
faq.answer: No, links are purely relational and don't affect the underlying workflow or status transitions of either ticket.

faq.question: Can I remove a link later without deleting the ticket?
faq.answer: Yes, links can be removed independently at any time without affecting either linked ticket's existence.
